# copilot.md — CBHN (California Black Health Network) Context

## Who CBHN is
CBHN = **California Black Health Network**, a nonprofit/community health organization focused on **Black health equity** in California. Their work sits at the intersection of:
- behavioral health + mental health
- health access & policy
- community-based support
- public awareness + stigma reduction
- convenings/events to educate, mobilize, and fundraise
- website: https://yourcbhn.org/

They produce forums and conferences that bring together:
- behavioral health professionals
- policymakers / public health officials
- community-based organizations
- researchers & data analysts
- funders / donors / prospective sponsors
- people with lived experience

Tone & brand intent: **professional, trustworthy, culturally grounded, clear and accessible**, not “corporate-techy”. They want materials that feel funder-ready.

---

## My role (Gabriel / MyWebGlory)
I support CBHN as an external creative + web/marketing partner.

Typical deliverables for CBHN:
- sponsorship documents / decks (fundraising assets)
- event flyers + social media graphics
- web pages / microsites for campaigns and reports
- polishing written content into polished, designed assets
- visual storytelling of impact (infographics, executive summaries, etc.)

Important: I often repurpose existing CBHN branding/assets to keep consistency and speed.

---

## Event: CBHN Health Equity Forum (HR1)
**Title:** CBHN Health Equity Forum: *Navigating HR1: What’s Changing, What’s Next, and How to Stay Covered*  
**Topic:** Impact of HR1 (“One Big Beautiful Bill Act”) on healthcare:
- access, availability, affordability
- changes in healthcare costs
- coverage shifts
- what to expect in 2026–2027
- anticipated changes affecting Black Californians and other vulnerable communities
**Format:** interactive discussion + Q&A  
**Featured Speaker:** **Dr. Monica Soni**, Chief Medical Officer, Covered California (Covered CA)  
**Purpose:** educate, prepare community, encourage advocacy, and drive registrations

**Date/Time (local to Vietnam shown in the invite):**
- Mar 27, 2026, 02:00 AM (Vietnam time)
(Original materials also referenced March 26, 2026 12–1pm; treat timezone carefully.)

Assets typically needed for this event:
- 1 primary flyer
- a small set of social assets (speaker spotlight, announcement, teaser, reminder)
- consistent use of CBHN + Covered CA logos

---

## Event: Virtual Behavioral & Mental Health Conference (May 2026)
**Title:** *Hidden Crises—Stress, Mental Health & Brain Health in the Black Community*  
**Date & Time:** Wednesday, **May 13, 2026**, 8am–1pm  
**Target audience (broad):**
- behavioral health professionals
- policymakers
- community members
- individuals with lived experience

**Audience segments (detailed):**
- Behavioral Health Professionals providing culturally responsive care in under-resourced communities
- Community-Based Organizations serving Black, Latinx, immigrant, low-income communities
- Public Health Officials / County Health Departments focused on disparities & delivery
- Researchers / Data Analysts focused on racial disparities, chronic stress physiology, cognitive health outcomes
- State & Local Policymakers working on behavioral health initiatives

### Core themes
- impact of chronic stress on mental + brain health
- suicide prevention for Black men and boys
- connection between mental health + cognitive decline (Alzheimer’s/dementia)
- community-driven approaches to healing, resilience, support
- breaking stigma + raising awareness

### Agenda (high level)
- 8:00 — Welcome & Keynote
- 9:00 — Toxic Stress
- 10:00 — Brain Health
- 11:00 — Suicide Among Black Men and Boys
- 12:00–12:25 — From Crisis to Policy: How HR1 Is Reshaping Black Mental Health
- 12:30 — Closing Keynote

### Speakers (known)
- Welcome + Keynote (8–8:50am):
  - Rhonda Smith (ED, CBHN)
  - Dr. Curley Bonds (Chief Medical Officer, LA County Dept. of Mental Health)
- Workshop 1: Toxic Stress (9–9:50am):
  - Dr. Tina Houston Armstrong (CEO, Living Connected Psychological, Inc.; Licensed Clinical Psychologist) — CEU
- Workshop 2: Brain Health (10–10:50am):
  - Dr. Monica Caffey (Academic Program Director & Professor, MA Counseling Psychology, National University)
- Workshop 3: Suicide Among Black Men and Boys (11–11:50am):
  - Mr. Kris Bennett (Director, Prostate Cancer Health Equity Initiatives, November)
- Workshop 4 (12–12:25pm): From Crisis to Policy: How HR1 Is Reshaping Black Mental Health
  - George Woods, MD (Felton Institute Board Chair)
- Closing Keynote (12:30–12:50pm):
  - Dr. Le Ondra Clark Harvey (CEO, California Behavioral Health Association — CBHA)

### Registration fees (as provided)
- $50 — General Admission (Early Bird & Members)
- $75 — Non-Members
- $95 — General Admission + 1 CEU
- $115 — CEU Package (1 CEU + Replay Access + Materials/Handouts)

## Current project track (latest request)
They previously purchased a **$700 sponsorship document** from me.

They are now requesting an **Impact Annual Report**:
- wants it to look very professional (like an example they shared)
- chronicle CBHN’s impact over the past **4 decades**
- use as a **fundraising tool** for funders / prospective donors
- wants an **infographic**
- wants an **executive summary**
They provided content already written: a ~37-page Word document.

My recommended direction: **a web-based report** (single-page longform, responsive) that can be published on CBHN’s domain, plus downloadable assets (exec summary PDF + infographic).

---

## Writing/Design guidelines for CBHN outputs
- Clarity over jargon; donor- and community-readable language
- Respectful, culturally aware, non-sensational framing (even for heavy topics)
- Strong hierarchy: headlines, sections, pull-quotes, key stats
- Avoid clutter; professional nonprofit look
- People-first visual storytelling (community imagery, multi-generational)
- California context is a plus when relevant (subtle cues)

---

## Operational assumptions for the agent (what to do by default)
When asked to create CBHN assets:
1. Reuse existing CBHN branding/logos and keep visual continuity.
2. Keep copy concise, structured, and fundraising-friendly.
3. Always sanity-check dates/timezones and restate in a clear format.
4. Prefer web-first deliverables with optional downloads for sharing/printing.
5. Suggest 2–4 fixed asset variants instead of open-ended “more graphics”.

---

## Helpful shortcuts / snippets
### Event positioning sentence (template)
“CBHN is convening this forum to help Black Californians and other vulnerable communities understand what’s changing, what to expect next, and how to stay covered—while creating space for questions, clarity, and advocacy.”

### CTAs (template)
- “Register to attend”
- “Save your seat”
- “Join the live discussion”
- “Get informed. Prepare for changes. Advocate for your health.”
